content: Date: 2026-05-20. I'm Sam, 31, backend/ML engineer at the Paris logistics startup (we're five engineers: me, Nora, Marc, Leïla, Jonas). I need to think out loud about on-call culture and what a humane rotation looks like for us — concrete, numbers, timeline. Current state: 7-day rotations, everyone on call about every 5 weeks, on-call window 18:00–09:00 weekdays + full weekends, average ~3 pages/week when I'm on call. Current on-call pay is €200/week. CTO Marion wants a proposal by 2026-06-15. I can't keep sacrificing dinner with Emma (2 years). Goal: a rotation that preserves sleep and family time, keeps system reliability, and is sustainable until we can hire an SRE (budget approval targeted for 2026-07-15, hire start earliest 2026-09). What concrete options do I have? I want trial dates, KPIs, and clear handover rules. Also: how to guard against noise and noisy alerts during dinner.
Which constraint is non-negotiable: family time, max pages, or cost?

Family time is non-negotiable — je veux être présent pour Emma's dinner. So practical constraints: Max pages per night should be 2, MTA target 15 min, MTTR target 90 min, weekends max 4 total pages. Cost constraint: can increase on-call pay a bit for quality-of-life changes but not more than +€150/week per person right now. Timeline constraint: Marion wants a plan by 2026-06-15 and I'd like to pilot before summer. Concrete pilot idea: run a 4‑week pilot 2026-06-01 to 2026-06-28. Pilot changes: 1) Protected dinner window 19:00–20:30 with pages suppressed unless Severity 1; 2) Strict severity routing: Sev1 SMS + Slack, Sev2 Slack mention only; 3) Weekend pair coverage (two people share weekend to reduce single-person burden) rotated so each person does 1 weekend every 10 weeks instead of every 5; 4) Comp: keep €200/week for pilot but give 1 paid comp day off if on-call week has >3 pages. Collect metrics weekly: pages per on-call, MTA, MTTR, number of Sev1s, and subjective sleep rating 1–5. Who does what: I configure alert routing, Nora owns runbooks, Leïla runs a 60‑minute training 2026-05-28 to align the team. If metrics worsen by >20% we revert. Does that cover it, or am I missing something?
Do you want the dinner window enforced technically (alert routing) or by policy/social rule, or both?

Both. Tech: add a severity tag to alerts; route only Sev1 to SMS during 19:00–20:30. Implementation detail: alerts need a numeric severity field — set threshold so CPU > 90% for >5 minutes is Sev2, memory OOM is Sev1, database unavailability is Sev1. I'll implement routing by 2026-05-27. Social: team agreement that non-Sev1 Slack pings during dinner are deferred unless the on-call explicitly asks for help. I'll propose this to Marion and ask her to back the social rule — cultural enforcement matters. Also: handover template — include current incidents, ongoing mitigations, estimated stability score (0–10), and on-call notes; handover done via a 10-min async note plus a 5-min live call if there are active mitigations. Handover doc ready by 2026-05-30. Any obvious holes?
Sounds good — how will you measure 'noise' reduction quantitatively in the pilot?

Measure noise by pages that are Sev2 or lower during 19:00–20:30 that generated a manual intervention — target 0 during dinner window. Also count Slack pings to on-call during that window (goal: <2/week). For the pilot dashboard: weekly numbers for pages, MTA, MTTR, Sev1 count, number of compensatory days triggered, and average sleep rating. Present dashboard to Marion on 2026-07-01. Contingencies: if average sleep rating drops below 3 or pages/night >2 for more than 2 consecutive weeks we pause the pilot and revert to previous rotation immediately. Budget: if pilot shows improvement we can argue for €100–150/week increase and hiring SRE with budget request by 2026-07-15. I also want a fallback: an external provider for DB incidents during nights for critical failures at €1,000/month as stopgap; include cost-benefit in proposal.
Who will own the dashboard and weekly review during the pilot?

I'll own the dashboard and weekly review; Nora will collect runbook updates and log handovers; Jonas will rotate as weekend co-owner with me for the first two weekends of the pilot, Marc takes the third, Leïla the fourth — written schedule by 2026-05-25. Communication plan: email to Marion and team on 2026-05-21 with pilot plan, and a 30‑minute meeting 2026-05-26 to walk through runbooks and the handover template. Decision points: 1) Approve pilot on 2026-05-21; 2) Start pilot 2026-06-01–06-28; 3) Review 2026-07-01; 4) Hire decision by 2026-07-15. I want to keep metrics simple and the rollback criteria clear. Anything else to lock down?
Lock the escalation SLA: how soon must on-call escalate to a second responder or manager for unresolved Sev1s?

Escalation rules: acknowledge Sev1 within 15 minutes; if unresolved after 30 minutes escalate to on-call lead (Nora) by phone; if unresolved after 90 minutes escalate to Marion. Document this in the runbook and automate escalation via PagerDaemon by 2026-05-27. Also add a post-incident check-in: within 72 hours the team debriefs and decides if an action is needed to avoid recurrence. Final decisions I want to take to Marion on 2026-06-15: pilot dates 2026-06-01–28, KPIs (MTA 15m, MTTR 90m, pages ≤2/night, weekends ≤4), dinner window 19:00–20:30 Sev1-only, pair weekend coverage, comp rule (keep €200/week + 1 comp day if >3 pages), dashboard owned by me, runbooks by Nora by 2026-05-28, training 2026-05-28, revert if metrics worsen >20% or sleep <3. Ready to draft the email and slide with timeline and KPIs.
Want me to draft the email to Marion and a one-slide summary with KPIs and timeline now?
